name: Dave Bartholomew
description: American musician, bandleader, composer, arranger, and record producer (1918–2019)
abstract: David Louis Bartholomew (December 24, 1918 – June 23, 2019) was an American musician, bandleader, composer, arranger, and record producer. He was prominent in the music of New Orleans throughout the second half of the 20th century.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Harris
about_quotes.evidence: Well the time I was playing with Celestan, they had a trumpet. We had Andrew Anderson, Dave Bartholomew, and probably Celestan.

about_quotes.speaker: Clarence Ford
about_quotes.evidence: I started working, I was working with Dave Bartholomew also. And I started making recordings with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Allen Toussaint
about_quotes.evidence: I played the piano for Dave, and I may have assisted some, but Dave was actually the producer there.

about_quotes.speaker: Cosimo Matassa
about_quotes.evidence: Ridgely did Tra-la-la, and Dave and I did it, and leased it to Decca Records.

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin "Red" Tyler
about_quotes.evidence: I got in the Dave Bartholomew's band... And I played that with Dave later.

about_quotes.speaker: Wallace Davenport
about_quotes.evidence: he always wanted to get a band together and have Dave Bartholomew himself and me, for the three trumpet players. But he never did get it together. Because I always had my band and Dave had his band.
